textPlain: >  on the van Emde Boas constructionCool to see that the capitalization of Dutch names was as confusing then as it is now.The correct way to write that would have been with a capital 'V'. The rule for these surname prefixes in The Netherlands is that they are not capitalized unless it's the first letter of the name. So it's "Peter van Emde Boas" but "Mr. Van Emde Boas".To add to the confusion, that capitalization rule is different for Flemish names. There the first one in the surname is always capitalized: "Willy Van der Steen" and "Mr. Van der Steen".
What was the piece of furniture?
